name: Suspicious Scheduled Task Detection
description: Detects and responds to suspicious scheduled task creation on managed endpoints. When a new scheduled task is created, the playbook analyzes the task properties for persistence indicators (hidden tasks, encoded payloads, system-level execution). Riggs AI performs a lateral movement assessment to determine if the task is part of a broader attack. Confirmed malicious tasks are removed, the host is isolated, and the SOC is notified. Legitimate tasks are documented.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: CrowdStrike Falcon
required_integrations.reason: Query scheduled task details, remove malicious tasks, and isolate endpoint

config.description: Triggers when a suspicious scheduled task creation event is detected on a managed endpoint
Analyze the scheduled task for persistence indicators including run level, payload encoding, and binary usage

config.additional_instructions: Analyze this scheduled task creation for persistence indicators. Determine if this is attacker-created persistence (MITRE T1053) or legitimate system administration. Consider the task creator, run level, and action binary.
config.description: AI-powered analysis of the scheduled task to determine if it represents attacker persistence
Routes based on whether the AI analysis determined the scheduled task is malicious persistence
Remove the malicious scheduled task from the endpoint via the EDR agent
Isolate the endpoint to prevent further attacker activity pending full investigation
Alert the SOC team with scheduled task details and containment actions taken

parameters.note: Scheduled task '{{code_1.task_name}}' on {{trigger_1.alert.hostname}} reviewed. Determined to be legitimate by {{code_1.creator}}. Flags: {{code_1.flags}}.

config.description: Document that the scheduled task is legitimate and close the alert
Suspicious scheduled task detection workflow complete
